book_claims.claim: Octopus is a Longevity Diet seafood featured in the traditional dish polpo e patate. It provides a small but meaningful amount of omega-3 DHA (approximately 0.13 g per 75 g cooked) and is a lean protein source.
book_claims.mechanism: Low-fat, high-protein marine food with modest omega-3 contribution; supports muscle maintenance without the saturated fat burden of land animal proteins.

supplementary_research.finding: Cephalopods including octopus are consumed regularly in Mediterranean Blue Zone regions and contribute to the characteristic high seafood intake pattern associated with reduced cardiovascular and all-cause mortality.

supplementary_research.source: Ozogul & Ozogul, Food Chemistry (2007) — PMID unavailable; DOI 10.1016/j.foodchem.2006.01.002
supplementary_research.finding: Octopus provides taurine (a conditionally essential amino acid), zinc, selenium, and vitamin B12 in meaningful amounts; taurine has anti-aging properties demonstrated in animal models and epidemiological studies.

supplementary_research.source: Kumar Singh et al., Advances in Nutrition (2023) — PMID 37003581
supplementary_research.finding: Taurine, abundant in cephalopods and other seafood, is identified as a longevity molecule: its blood levels decline with aging; supplementation extended lifespan and healthspan in multiple animal models, suggesting dietary taurine from seafood is a relevant longevity factor.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Complete protein with high digestibility; very low in saturated fat, making it a preferred animal protein in longevity-oriented diets.
Conditionally essential sulfur amino acid; high bioavailability from animal sources; may protect against age-related cellular damage.
Marine long-chain omega-3; directly incorporated into neuronal membranes; lower per 100 g than oily fish but still a useful contributor in a varied seafood diet.
Very high B12 concentration; a single serving substantially covers daily requirements.

synergies.description: Traditional polpo e patate pairing provides complete amino acids, complex carbohydrates, potassium, and omega-3 in a single dish aligned with Blue Zone Calabrian cuisine.
Olive oil's monounsaturated fats and polyphenols complement octopus's lean protein and trace omega-3, forming a heart-protective combination.
Parsley vitamin C enhances iron absorption from octopus; the pairing is a culinary classic in Mediterranean seafood cooking.
